title: Timeline of the Past
description: Visualize how the past perfect is placed BEFORE the past simple.

items.label: ⏮️ Past Perfect
items.labelEn: had + past participle — what had happened BEFORE

items.label: 📏 Subject + HAD + past participle
items.labelEn: Same for ALL subjects: I/you/he/she/it/we/they had ('d)

items.label: 📅 Timeline
items.labelEn: 1st: She HAD LEFT (Past Perfect) → 2nd: I arrived (Past Simple) → NOW

items.label: ✅ When I arrived...
items.labelEn: ...she HAD already LEFT. (ella se fue PRIMERO)

items.label: ✅ By the time we got there...
🔗 Connectors
items.labelEn: when, before, after, by the time, already, never...before
I HADN'T finished. HAD you finished? Yes, I had.

items.label: ⚖️ PS vs PP
items.labelEn: He arrived and she left (seq.) vs. When he arrived, she HAD left (she left FIRST)

title: Past Perfect: Conjugation
description: Practice forming the past perfect correctly.

cards.back: When I arrived, she had left.

cards.front: They ___ (finish) dinner before the movie started.
cards.back: They had finished dinner before the movie started.

cards.front: I ___ never ___ (see) snow until I visited Canada.
cards.back: I had never seen snow until I visited Canada.

cards.front: By the time we got there, the train ___ (leave).
cards.back: By the time we got there, the train had left.
cards.example: The train left BEFORE we arrived.

cards.front: She ___ (not / eat) breakfast, so she was hungry.
cards.back: She hadn't eaten breakfast, so she was hungry.
Had you done your homework before class?

cards.front: After he ___ (read) the book, he returned it.
cards.back: After he had read the book, he returned it.

cards.front: I ___ (already / buy) the tickets when she called.
cards.back: I had already bought the tickets when she called.

title: Conector → Ejemplo
description: Match each connector with its correct example.

pairs.right: When I arrived, she had left.
I had finished before the deadline.
After she had eaten, she left.
By the time we got there, it had started.
I had never tried sushi before.

title: Sequence of Events
description: Sort to show what happened first.

sentences.prompt: When I arrived, she was already gone:
sentences.correct: When I arrived, she had already left

sentences.prompt: They were finished before the movie started:
sentences.correct: They had finished before the movie started
I had never seen snow before

sentences.prompt: By the time we arrived, the train had left:
sentences.correct: By the time we arrived, the train had left

sentences.prompt: I hadn't had breakfast, so I was hungry:
sentences.correct: She hadn't eaten breakfast, so she was hungry

title: Quiz: Pasado Perfecto
description: Demonstrate your mastery of the Past Perfect.

questions.question: "When I arrived, she ___ (leave)."
questions.explanation: She left BEFORE I arrived → Past Perfect.

questions.question: "By the time we got there, the concert ___ (start)."
"She ___ eaten breakfast, so she was hungry."
"___ you done your homework before class?"
questions.explanation: Question in Past Perfect: Had + subject.

questions.question: "After he had ___ the book, he returned it." (read)
Which sentence shows Past Perfect correctly?
options: I had already bought the tickets when she called.
I have already bought the tickets when she called.
I already bought the tickets when she called.
I was already buying the tickets when she called.
questions.explanation: Past Perfect: had already + past participle.

title: Tiempos Perfectos
description: Unscramble past and future perfect words.
